description: Repository-level contributor concentration and bus-factor indicators.
caveat: Computed from committed contributor snapshots; merged identities depend on the maintainer-reviewed map.

description: Slim commit snapshot by repository, SHA, date, author, and subject.
caveat: Author names/emails reflect Git metadata in the snapshot and should not be treated as a curated identity authority.

description: Maintainer-reviewed contributor identity and ORCID readiness summary.
caveat: Identity fields are conservative; unknown or unconfirmed identities remain unresolved until the maintainer confirms them.

description: Repository/login contribution counts from the GitHub snapshot.
caveat: Counts are GitHub contribution signals, not a complete scholarly credit model.

description: Base OBS-T correction-event table before later component and release enrichments.
caveat: Intermediate research artifact; cite the release table for stable OBS-T reuse.

description: Metadata sidecar for the base OBS-T correction-event table.
caveat: Sidecar describes the local generated artifact and should be read with the matching CSV.

description: Combined OBS-T event table across form and git-derived correction layers.
caveat: Intermediate aggregate; use the release CSV for the citable public dataset.

description: Final typed OBS-T event table before release split annotation.
caveat: Intermediate artifact retained for auditability; release-specific consumers should use correction_events_release.csv.

description: Metadata sidecar for correction_events_final.csv.
caveat: Sidecar describes the generated final table and should be read with the matching CSV.

description: Git-derived OBS-T correction events mined from dictionary entry files.
caveat: Git reconstruction is conservative and should be audited through source_path and commit_sha.

description: Metadata sidecar for git-derived OBS-T correction events.
caveat: Sidecar reports reconstruction scope and hunk accounting for the matching CSV.

description: Citable OBS-T release table with typed correction events and split labels.
caveat: Released identity fields use the repository identity policy; raw private contact data is not a release interface.

description: Metadata sidecar for the citable OBS-T release table.
caveat: Read with correction_events_release.csv for schema, counts, and release caveats.

description: OBS-T correction events with component and attribution-route annotations.
caveat: Intermediate table; component attribution includes conservative and fuzzy routes that require caveat-aware interpretation.

description: Metadata sidecar for correction_events_typed.csv.
caveat: Sidecar includes attribution-route counts and should be read with the matching CSV.

description: Catalog of public CSV/JSON data files with source scripts, generated dates, and caveats.
caveat: Generated from tracked public data files; use it as the site index, not as a data source for analysis.

description: Chapman capture-recapture estimates of error-prone records remaining per dictionary, from two-era overlap.
caveat: Order-of-magnitude only: sequential occasions and heterogeneous catchability violate Chapman assumptions in opposite directions; estimates capped at record counts.

description: Scholar-framed external reach: GitHub stars/forks, 14-day clone/view traffic for core repos, downstream dependents (known consumers + code-search hits), and representative citations, in one tidy long table.
caveat: Mixed measured/estimated: traffic is a sliding 14-day sample of core repos (not all 76); dependents from code search are a floor; citations are representative, not exhaustive. Zenodo tier is blocked pending a DOI correction.

description: Backlog age pyramid: currently-open issues bucketed by age, with silent (zero-comment) counts.
caveat: Ages are computed at the snapshot as-of date, not at page-view time.

description: Time-to-close percentiles (median/p25/p75/p90) per closing year, issues and PRs separately.
caveat: Closed-at is GitHub close time; PRs are few (89 total) so annual PR rows are small samples.

description: Per-repository responsiveness: open/closed counts, close-latency percentiles, open-age median, silent-open count.
caveat: Latency percentiles are blank for repos with fewer than 20 closed issues.

description: Cohort survival: % of each opening-year cohort still open after 30/90/180/365/730/1460 days.
caveat: Right-censored: a cohort only reports horizons its issues have had time to reach at the snapshot date.

description: Annual issue counts by taxonomy/type label.
caveat: Labels reflect the current taxonomy snapshot; historical issue labels may have been normalized after the fact.

description: Slim issue and pull-request snapshot used by issue and taxonomy dashboards.
caveat: Labels are pipe-separated and may combine type, severity, workflow, and legacy labels.

description: Snapshot manifest with data row counts and the current observatory snapshot date.
caveat: Counts describe the committed transformed files; rerun the refresh pipeline before citing newer live GitHub state.

description: Phase-3 hypothesis rigor metrics (H4-H8): specialisation, edit-type invariance, and related tests with permutation p-values.
caveat: Descriptive rigor statistics over the current release split; permutation p-values shift when the release or feature set changes.

description: Annual correction-sustainability aggregates by commits, correctors, and leading contributor share.
caveat: Probe-level OBS-Q artifact; it summarizes process signals, not dictionary-content quality.

description: Correction issue open/close latency inputs.
caveat: Latency depends on GitHub issue closure practice and may not equal actual correction deployment time.

description: Per-dictionary correction-sustainability aggregates.
caveat: Dictionary-level process signal; small dictionaries and burst campaigns can dominate rates.

description: OBS-T baseline model metrics for detection, correction, and location classification.
caveat: Baseline metrics are descriptive and depend on the current release split and feature set.

description: Documented OBS-T correction campaigns by date, magnitude, category, and dictionaries.
caveat: Campaign detection is rule-based and intended for context, not as a complete editorial history.

description: OBS-T component counts by layer and dictionary.
caveat: Component labels are typology abstractions and should be interpreted with the datasheet.

description: OBS-T edit confusion counts by changed unit, layer, and edit space.
caveat: Raw source-space edits are separated from Sanskrit-bearing edit spaces where possible.

description: OBS-T release-safe corrector summary by alias/name, event count, component, and date span.
caveat: Identity display follows the release identity policy and should not be used to deanonymize contributors.

description: OBS-T corrector-by-component event counts for release-safe contributor/component analysis.
caveat: Identity display follows the release identity policy and should not be used to deanonymize contributors.

description: Crosswalk counts from OBS-T components into external classification schemes.
caveat: Crosswalks are approximate mappings for comparison, not replacement labels.

description: Dictionary-level OBS-T event density and dominant component summary.
caveat: Entry denominators and event density are best used for comparison, not ranking dictionary quality.

description: OBS-T hypothesis statistics, effect sizes, bootstrap checks, and trend summaries.
caveat: Statistical tests are descriptive for the generated corpus and should be cited with limitations.

description: OBS-T robustness and sensitivity checks.
caveat: Sensitivity outputs are diagnostic and should be read with the main rigor report.

description: OBS-T silver-validation summary.
caveat: Silver validation is automated and does not replace the human-gated gold/error samples.

description: OBS-T headline counts and distribution summaries used by the site.
caveat: Summary is derived from the current generated tables and changes when the release pipeline is rerun.

description: Annual OBS-T event counts by layer and component.
caveat: Timeline reflects event dates available in source layers and documented campaigns.

description: Monthly OBS-T event counts by component.
caveat: Month-level trends are sensitive to large campaigns and should be filtered by component/layer where relevant.

description: OBS-T transliteration validation and sensitivity summary.
caveat: Validation compares conventions and includes known sibilant/convention ambiguity caveats.

description: Curated people summary used by contributor dashboards.
caveat: ORCID and identity fields depend on maintainer review and contributor consent.

description: Summary of PWG <ls> citation link coverage: scan vs HTML targets over the translated article subset.
caveat: Derived in the PWG repo, not regenerated here; see reports/pwg_citation_coverage.md for the live sources.

description: Repository health flags for license, branch, activity, description, and cleanup risk.
caveat: Repository-health flags are decision support; external repo changes still require maintainer approval.

description: Repository metadata-completeness snapshot for README, citation, templates, workflows, releases, Dependabot, and CodeQL.
caveat: Live fields depend on GitHub API access; check fetch_warning and unknown flags after each refresh.

description: Repository inventory with description, language, size, dates, branch, license, and archive state.
caveat: Snapshot excludes external facts not exposed by GitHub repository metadata.

description: Org-wide statistics census register (H817/ROADMAP_STATISTICS_ORG_CENSUS): one row per Part-0 headline statistic across the 7 data layers (lexicon text, morphology, corpus, translation, roots, repo-meta, product), each with its computed value, done/partial/not_started status, owning repo, and source link.
caveat: Values are aggregated from ~15 sibling repos, not computed in this repo; re-verify against the linked source before citing a specific count. as_of_date is per-row, not a single snapshot date.

description: Annual taxonomy adoption metrics: typed, one-type, severity, milestone, and conformance rates.
caveat: Conformance is measured against the current taxonomy rules and may change as labels are cleaned.

description: Annual repository activity aggregates for issues, pull requests, commits, and authors.
caveat: Aggregates come from the committed GitHub snapshot and inherit its refresh date.

description: Monthly repository activity aggregates for issues, pull requests, commits, and authors.
caveat: Monthly buckets support operational trends and should not be over-read for inactive or archived repos.

description: Organization-level yearly velocity and backlog summary.
caveat: Backlog is based on issue open/close state in the snapshot and can change after label/issue cleanup.

description: Repository workflow/release reliability baseline covering workflows, scheduled jobs, artifact refresh, Dependabot, CodeQL, and releases.
caveat: Workflow classification is read-only and keyword-based for artifact/deploy/refresh categories; fetch warnings should be reviewed before citing.
